chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يَحْتَجِمُ أَوْ يَقُصُّ ظُفُراً أَوْ شَعْراً أَوْ شَيْئاً مِنْهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 95 – The one in Ihraam gets cupping done, or he clips nails, or hair, or something from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/322)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/322)
text: لَا يَأْخُذِ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنْ شَعْرِ الْحَلَالِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muawiya Bin Ammar,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The one in Ihraam cannot take (cut) from the hairs of one not in Ihraam’.
en.sarwar: 7. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One in the state of Ihram must not cut any hair of al- Hallal (one who is not in the state of Ihram).”’
